sections.heading: Unsupported Features
sections.body:
- You cannot create indexes on federated data sources.

- Supported authentication methods are limited to [SCRAM](manual/core/security-scram), [X.509 Certificates](manual/core/security-x.509), [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization), and [AWS IAM Authentication](set-up-pwdless-auth) .

- Atlas monitoring tools do not cover Federated Database Instances.

- The federated database instance can use object storage from only *one cloud provider* and from only *one account* within that provider.

- Change streams and multi-document ACID transactions are not supported on federated sources.

sections.heading: Document Size and Result Ordering
sections.body:
- Federated database instances don't support documents larger than 16 MB.

- Returned document order is not guaranteed unless enforced (for example, with `$sort`).

sections.heading: Source and Region Considerations
sections.body:
- Each federated database instance runs in a specific cloud and region.

- Archived data stays in the provider's private network.

- Object storage sources are *read-only*.

sections.heading: Client Connections per Region
sections.body: - Up to *120 guaranteed simultaneous client connections* per region are supported.

sections.heading: Concurrent Queries
sections.body:
- Running more than *30 simultaneous queries* on an federated database instance is not supported.

If your aggregation pipeline only contains the [$currentOp](determine-query-status) stage, Atlas Data Federation doesn't enforce the limit on
the maximum number of simultaneous queries. You can run queries that
only contain the [$currentOp](determine-query-status) stage even
after you reach the maximum number of simultaneous queries.

sections.heading: Query Timeout
sections.body:
Queries sent to MongoDB Atlas Data Federation have a time limit of 6 hours. If
[maxTimeMS](atlas-ui-query-bar-max-time-ms) is set up, then the query
timeout should be the minimum of the these two values, for
example: `min(maxTimeMS, 6 hours)`.

sections.heading: Instance Count per Project
sections.body:
- Each project supports up to *25 federated database instances* by default.

- Contact your product representative to increase the limit to up to *100 federated database instances*

sections.heading: Provider-Specific Recomendations
sections.body:
**Aws:**AWS| Supported storage | Amazon S3 |
| ----------------- | --------- |**Azure:**Microsoft Azure| Supported storage | Blob Storage |
| ----------------- | ------------ |**Gcp:**Google Cloud| Supported storage | Google Cloud Storage. |
| ----------------- | --------------------- |
